creator: cmt@freebsd.org
text:
Thunderbird search uses sqlite3 behind the scenes. In particular, it uses the fts3_tokenizer() in it's two-argument incarnation - the very sqlite3 feature which was made optional in ports r411118 - see also linked https://www.sqlite.org/compile.html#enable_fts3_tokenizer

Until someone (me? you? upstream?) has a better idea, the workaround is to rebuild databases/sqlite3 with FTS3_TOKEN enabled (should that become default, despite it's security concerns? I'd rather go for sqlite3 3.12 and activate this feature only on these particular connections).
